# zidl â€” C++ Backend Reference

Generated by `zidl -b cpp`. Produces a single `.hpp` header-only file containing
type declarations and inline CDR serialize/deserialize functions. Uses the IDL4-native
C++ mapping (formal/25-03-03, C++11 minimum).

---

## Generated Files

| File | Contents |
|---|---|
| `<name>.hpp` | Type declarations + inline CDR serialize/deserialize |

`--split-files` produces one `.hpp` per top-level named type.

The output is header-only: there is no `.cpp` source to compile separately. Serialize
and deserialize functions are inline definitions inside the header.

---

## Type Mapping

| IDL type | C++ type |
|---|---|
| `boolean` | `bool` |
| `octet` | `uint8_t` |
| `char` | `char` |
| `wchar` | `wchar_t` |
| `short` / `int16` | `int16_t` |
| `unsigned short` / `uint16` | `uint16_t` |
| `long` / `int32` | `int32_t` |
| `unsigned long` / `uint32` | `uint32_t` |
| `long long` / `int64` | `int64_t` |
| `unsigned long long` / `uint64` | `uint64_t` |
| `float` | `float` |
| `double` | `double` |
| `long double` | `long double` |
| `fixed<D,S>` | `double` |
| `string` (unbounded) | `std::string` |
| `string<N>` (bounded) | `zidl::BoundedString<N>` |
| `wstring` | `std::wstring` |
| `sequence<T>` | `std::vector<T>` |
| `T[N]` | `std::array<T, N>` |
| `map<K,V>` | `std::map<K, V>` |
| `@optional T` | `std::optional<T>` |
| `enum` | `enum class Foo : uint32_t { ... }` |
| `bitmask` | `enum class Foo : uint8/16/32/64_t { ... }` |
| `bitset` | `struct Foo` with integer storage member |
| `struct` | `struct Foo` (with `: public Base` for inheritance) |
| `union` | `class Foo` with `_d()` accessor + anonymous union |
| IDL module | C++ namespace |

---

## Generated File Structure

```cpp
#pragma once  // or #ifndef guard

#include <cstdint>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>
#include <array>
#include <map>
#include "zidl_cdr.h"

namespace MyModule {

struct MyStruct {
    int32_t x{};
    std::string name{};
    std::optional<double> opt_val{};
};

inline void zidl_serialize(ZidlCdrWriter *w, const MyStruct &v) { ... }
inline void zidl_deserialize(ZidlCdrReader *r, MyStruct &v, ZidlCdrAllocator *alloc) { ... }

// For keyed types:
inline int MyStruct_serialize_key(ZidlCdrWriter *w, const MyStruct &v) { ... }
inline int MyStruct_deserialize_key(ZidlCdrReader *r, MyStruct &v) { ... }
inline int MyStruct_compute_key_hash(const MyStruct &v, uint8_t hash[16]) { ... }

} // namespace MyModule
```

Use `--pragma-once` to emit `#pragma once` instead of the default `#ifndef` guards.
Use `--cpp-namespace <ns>` to wrap all output in an outer namespace.

---

## CDR Serialize/Deserialize Signatures

Serialize and deserialize are free functions overloaded per type:

```cpp
/* Serialize v into the CDR writer w. */
void zidl_serialize(ZidlCdrWriter *w, const Foo &v);

/* Deserialize from CDR reader r into v. alloc used for strings/sequences. */
void zidl_deserialize(ZidlCdrReader *r, Foo &v, ZidlCdrAllocator *alloc);
```

---

## Key Serialization

For structs with `@key` members:

```cpp
/* Serialize key fields only as canonical PLAIN_CDR2 big-endian */
int Foo_serialize_key(ZidlCdrWriter *w, const Foo &v);

/* Deserialize key fields from a full CDR stream; non-key fields skipped */
int Foo_deserialize_key(ZidlCdrReader *r, Foo &v);

/* Compute RTPS key hash: key â‰¤ 16 bytes â†’ zero-pad; > 16 bytes â†’ MD5 */
int Foo_compute_key_hash(const Foo &v, uint8_t hash[16]);
```

---

## `@verbatim` Injection (not yet implemented)

`@verbatim` annotations are parsed and preserved in the IR as raw annotations
(accessible via `TypeAnnotations.raw`), but the C++ backend does not yet read
or emit them. The planned placement positions are `BEGIN_FILE`,
`BEFORE_DECLARATION`, `BEGIN_DECLARATION`, `END_DECLARATION`,
`AFTER_DECLARATION`, and `END_FILE`, filtered by `language == "c++"` or
`language == "*"`.

---

## `zidl-cdr` Dependency

The generated `.hpp` includes `zidl_cdr.h` and uses `ZidlCdrWriter`, `ZidlCdrReader`,
`ZidlCdrAllocator`, `zidl_cdr_compute_key_hash`, and `zidl_md5` from `zidl-cdr`.

Build by including the header in your C++ project and compiling
`packages/zidl-cdr/src/zidl_cdr.c` as a C source file alongside your C++ files.
Add `packages/zidl-cdr/include` to your include paths.

---

## Backend-Specific Options

| Option | Effect |
|---|---|
| `--pragma-once` | Emit `#pragma once` instead of `#ifndef` guards |
| `--header-guard-prefix <pfx>` | Prefix for `#ifndef` include guards |
| `--cpp-namespace <ns>` | Wrap all output in an outer namespace |
| `--export-macro <macro>` | DLL export macro for topic descriptor declarations |
| `--no-typesupport` | Suppress CDR serialize/deserialize output |

---

## Known Limitations

| Feature | Status |
|---|---|
| `--pl-cdr` (PL_CDR emit) | Flag parsed but C++ backend does not yet emit PL_CDR functions |
| Union discriminant: complex types | Emits `/* TODO: unsupported discriminant */` |
| `--generate-interfaces`: complex-type adaptation | `emitImplOp` emits `/* TODO: adapt C++ types */` stubs (ABI boundary not yet decided) |
